en: Then Pilate took Jesus and flogged him.
zh: 那時，彼拉多吩咐人把耶穌拉去鞭打。

en: And the soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head and arrayed him in a purple robe.
zh: 士兵用荊棘編成冠冕，戴在他的頭上，又給他披上紫色的外袍，

en: They came up to him, saying, "Hail, King of the Jews!" and struck him with their hands.
zh: 然後來到他面前，說：“猶太人的王萬歲！”並且用手掌打他。

en: Pilate went out again and said to them, "See, I am bringing him out to you that you may know that I find no guilt in him."
zh: 彼拉多再次出到外面，對猶太人說：“看！我把他帶出來給你們，讓你們知道我查不出他有甚麼罪。”

en: So Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said to them, "Behold the man!"
zh: 於是耶穌出來，戴著荊棘的冠冕，披著紫色的外袍。彼拉多對他們說：“看，這個人！”

en: When the chief priests and the officers saw him, they cried out, "Crucify him, crucify him!" Pilate said to them, "Take him yourselves and crucify him, for I find no guilt in him."
zh: 祭司長和差役看見耶穌，就喊叫說：“把他釘十字架！把他釘十字架！”彼拉多對他們說：“你們自己把他帶去釘十字架吧！我查不出他有甚麼罪。”

en: The Jews answered him, "We have a law, and according to that law he ought to die because he has made himself the Son of God."
zh: 猶太人回答：“我們有律法，根據那律法，他是該死的，因為他自命為 神的兒子。”

en: When Pilate heard this statement, he was even more afraid.
zh: 彼拉多聽見這話，就更加害怕，

en: He entered his headquarters again and said to Jesus, "Where are you from?" But Jesus gave him no answer.
zh: 又進了官邸，問耶穌：“你究竟是從哪裡來的？”耶穌卻不回答他。

en: So Pilate said to him, "You will not speak to me? Do you not know that I have authority to release you and authority to crucify you?"
zh: 彼拉多對他說：“你不對我說話嗎？你不知道我有權釋放你，也有權把你釘十字架嗎？”

en: Jesus answered him, "You would have no authority over me at all unless it had been given you from above. Therefore he who delivered me over to you has the greater sin."
zh: 耶穌說：“如果不是從天上給你權柄，你就無權辦我；因此，把我交給你的那人，罪更重了。”

en: From then on Pilate sought to release him, but the Jews cried out, "If you release this man, you are not Caesar's friend. Everyone who makes himself a king opposes Caesar."
zh: 從那時起，彼拉多想釋放耶穌；可是猶太人卻喊叫說：“如果你釋放這個人，就不是凱撒的忠臣了。凡是自命為王的，就是與凱撒為敵。”

en: So when Pilate heard these words, he brought Jesus out and sat down on the judgment seat at a place called The Stone Pavement, and in Aramaic Gabbatha.
zh: 彼拉多聽了這些話，就把耶穌帶到外面，到了一個名叫“鋪石地” 的地方，就在那裡開庭審問。

en: Now it was the day of Preparation of the Passover. It was about the sixth hour. He said to the Jews, "Behold your King!"
zh: 那天是逾越節的預備日，約在正午的時候。彼拉多對猶太人說：“看，你們的王！”

en: They cried out, "Away with him, away with him, crucify him!" Pilate said to them, "Shall I crucify your King?" The chief priests answered, "We have no king but Caesar."
zh: 他們就喊叫起來：“除掉他！除掉他！把他釘十字架！”彼拉多問他們：“我可以把你們的王釘十字架嗎？”祭司長回答：“除了凱撒，我們沒有王！”

en: So he delivered him over to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus,
zh: 於是彼拉多把耶穌交給他們去釘十字架。

en: and he went out, bearing his own cross, to the place called the place of a skull, which in Aramaic is called Golgotha.
zh: 他們把耶穌帶去了。耶穌自己背著十字架出來，到了一個名叫“髑髏”的地方，希伯來話叫各各他。

en: There they crucified him, and with him two others, one on either side, and Jesus between them.
zh: 他們就在那裡把耶穌釘在十字架上；和他一同釘十字架的，還有兩個人，一邊一個，耶穌在中間。

en: Pilate also wrote an inscription and put it on the cross. It read, "Jesus of Nazareth, the King of the Jews."
zh: 彼拉多寫了一個牌子，放在十字架上頭，寫的是：“猶太人的王拿撒勒人耶穌。”

en: Many of the Jews read this inscription, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city, and it was written in Aramaic, in Latin, and in Greek.
zh: 有許多猶太人念了這牌子上所寫的，因為耶穌釘十字架的地方離城不遠，而且那牌子是用希伯來文、拉丁文和希臘文寫的。

en: So the chief priests of the Jews said to Pilate, "Do not write, 'The King of the Jews,' but rather, 'This man said, I am King of the Jews.'"
zh: 猶太人的祭司長對彼拉多說：“不要寫‘猶太人的王’，要寫‘這個人自稱：我是猶太人的王’。”

en: Pilate answered, "What I have written I have written."
zh: 彼拉多說：“我所寫的，我已經寫了！”

en: When the soldiers had crucified Jesus, they took his garments and divided them into four parts, one part for each soldier; also his tunic. But the tunic was seamless, woven in one piece from top to bottom,
zh: 士兵把耶穌釘了十字架之後，就把他的衣服拿來，分成四分，每個兵一分。他們又拿他的內衣；這內衣是沒有縫的，是從上到下整件織成的。

en: so they said to one another, "Let us not tear it, but cast lots for it to see whose it shall be." This was to fulfill the Scripture which says, "They divided my garments among them, and for my clothing they cast lots." So the soldiers did these things,
zh: 因此，他們彼此說：“我們不要把它撕開，我們來抽籤吧，看看是誰的。”這就應驗了經上所說的： “他們分了我的外衣， 又為我的內衣抽籤。” 士兵果然這樣作了。

en: but standing by the cross of Jesus were his mother and his mother's sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.
zh: 站在耶穌十字架旁邊的，有他母親和他母親的姊妹，還有高羅巴的妻子馬利亞，和抹大拉的馬利亞。

en: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, "Woman, behold, your son!"
zh: 耶穌看見母親，又看見他所愛的那門徒站在旁邊，就對母親說：“母親 ，看！你的兒子。”

en: Then he said to the disciple, "Behold, your mother!" And from that hour the disciple took her to his own home.
zh: 然後他對那門徒說：“看！你的母親。”從那時起，那門徒就把她接到自己的家裡去了。

en: After this, Jesus, knowing that all was now finished, said (to fulfill the Scripture), "I thirst."
zh: 這事以後，耶穌知道一切都已經成就了，為了要使經上的話應驗，就說：“我渴了。”

en: A jar full of sour wine stood there, so they put a sponge full of the sour wine on a hyssop branch and held it to his mouth.
zh: 在那裡有一個罈子，盛滿了酸酒，他們就拿海綿浸了酸酒，綁在牛膝草上，送到他的口裡。

en: When Jesus had received the sour wine, he said, "It is finished," and he bowed his head and gave up his spirit.
zh: 耶穌嘗了那酸酒，說：“成了！”就低下頭，斷了氣。

en: Since it was the day of Preparation, and so that the bodies would not remain on the cross on the Sabbath (for that Sabbath was a high day), the Jews asked Pilate that their legs might be broken and that they might be taken away.
zh: 因為那天是預備日，為了要避免屍體在安息日留在十字架上 ，猶太人就請求彼拉多打斷那些被釘十字架的人的腿，把他們拿下來。

en: So the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first, and of the other who had been crucified with him.
zh: 於是士兵來了，把和耶穌一同釘十字架的那兩個人的腿都先後打斷了。

en: But when they came to Jesus and saw that he was already dead, they did not break his legs.
zh: 他們來到耶穌那裡，看見他已經死了，就沒有打斷他的腿。

en: But one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and at once there came out blood and water.
zh: 但是有一個士兵用槍刺他的肋旁，立刻有血和水流出來。

en: He who saw it has borne witness- his testimony is true, and he knows that he is telling the truth- that you also may believe.
zh: 那看見這事的人已經作證了，他的見證是真實的，他也知道自己所說的是實在的，使你們也相信。

en: For these things took place that the Scripture might be fulfilled: "Not one of his bones will be broken."
zh: 這些事的發生，是要應驗經上所說的：“他的骨頭，一根也不可折斷。”

en: And again another Scripture says, "They will look on him whom they have pierced."
zh: 另有一處經文說：“他們要仰望自己所刺的人。”

en: After these things Joseph of Arimathea, who was a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, asked Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus, and Pilate gave him permission. So he came and took away his body.
zh: 這些事以後，有一個亞利馬太人約瑟來求彼拉多，要領耶穌的身體；他因為怕猶太人，就暗暗地作耶穌的門徒。彼拉多批准了，他便把耶穌的身體領去。

en: Nicodemus also, who earlier had come to Jesus by night, came bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about seventy-five pounds in weight.
zh: 從前夜間來見耶穌的尼哥德慕也來了，帶著沒藥和沉香混合的香料，約有三十二公斤。

en: So they took the body of Jesus and bound it in linen cloths with the spices, as is the burial custom of the Jews.
zh: 他們領取了耶穌的身體，照著猶太人的葬禮的規例，用細麻布和香料把他裹好。

en: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden, and in the garden a new tomb in which no one had yet been laid.
zh: 在耶穌釘十字架的地方，有一個園子，園裡有一個新的墓穴，是從來沒有葬過人的。

en: So because of the Jewish day of Preparation, since the tomb was close at hand, they laid Jesus there.
zh: 因為那天是猶太人的預備日，又因為那墓穴就在附近，他們就把耶穌葬在那裡。
